headline: Tuberculosis Incidence Remains High at 92 Per 100,000 Population
context: Malaysia's TB rate remains stubbornly high and is classified by WHO as a high-burden country for tuberculosis.

cards.big: TB incidence of 92 per 100,000 keeps Malaysia on WHO high-burden list in 2025
cards.sub: 29,000 new TB cases diagnosed with estimated 10,000 additional undetected cases

cards.big: TB treatment completion rate dropped to 78% from 85% in 2020 among foreign workers
cards.sub: Job loss and deportation fear causes treatment interruption breeding drug resistance

cards.big: Migrant worker TB screening covers initial employment but no follow-up screening exists
cards.sub: One-time screening misses the 40% of cases that develop after initial clearance

cards.big: Vietnam reduced TB incidence 25% through active case finding in high-risk communities
cards.sub: Proactive screening rather than passive detection accelerates case identification

cards.big: TB does not respect immigration status—untreated cases in any community threaten everyone
Should Malaysia provide free TB treatment to all residents including undocumented workers
